div1.entryFree: Saw, subst. a moral saying, a maxim, a sentence: who fears a sentence or an old man's s. Lucr. 244. coughing drowns the parson's s. LLL V, 2, 932. full of wise --s and modern instances, As II, 7, 156. now I find thy s. of might, III, 5, 82. we'll whisper o'er a couplet or two of most sage --s, Tw. III, 4, 413. his weapons holy saws of sacred writ, H6B I, 3, 61. all --s of books, Hml. I, 5, 100. that must approve the common s. Lr. II, 2, 167.
